questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: The area (in square units) of the region enclosed between the parabola $y^2=2 x$ and the line $y=4 x-1$
questions.solution:
Given equation of parabola $y^2=2 x$ $\ldots(\mathrm{i})$ and equation of line $y=4 x-1$ $\ldots(\mathrm{ii})$ Intersecting points of Eqs. (i) and (ii) $\operatorname{are}\left(\frac{1}{8}, \frac{-1}{2}\right)$ and $\left(\frac{1}{2}, 1\right)$
Required area $A O B$ $=\int_{-1 / 2}^1\left(\frac{y+1}{4}-\frac{y^2}{2}\right) d y$ $=\frac{1}{4} \int_{-1 / 2}^1(y+1) d y-\frac{1}{2} \int_{-1 / 2}^1 y^2 d y$ $=\frac{1}{4}\left[\frac{y^2}{2}+y\right]_{-1 / 2}^1-\frac{1}{2}\left[\frac{y^3}{3}\right]_{-1 / 2}^1$ $=\frac{1}{4}\left[\left(\frac{1}{2}+1\right)-\left(\frac{1}{8}-\frac{1}{2}\right)\right]-\frac{1}{6}\left[1-\left(-\frac{1}{8}\right)\right]$ $=\frac{1}{4}\left[\frac{3}{2}+\frac{3}{8}\right]-\frac{1}{6}\left(\frac{9}{8}\right)=\frac{1}{4} \times \frac{15}{8}-\frac{3}{16}$ $=\frac{15-6}{32}=\frac{9}{32}$ sq units
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: The area of the region bounded by the curves $y=9 x^2$ and $y=5 x^2+4$ (in sq units) is
questions.solution:
Intersecting point of $y=9 x^2$ and $y=5 x^2+4$ is given by
$$ \begin{aligned} & \Rightarrow \quad 4 x^2=4 \\ & \Rightarrow \quad x^2=1 \\ & \Rightarrow \quad x= \pm 1 \\ & \therefore \quad y=9( \pm 1)^2=9 \\ & \end{aligned} $$ Hence, intersecting points are $( \pm 1,9)$. $$ \begin{aligned} \therefore \text { Required area } & =2 \int_0^1\left[\left(5 x^2+4\right)-\left(9 x^2\right)\right] d x \\ & =2 \int_0^1\left(4-4 x^2\right) d x \\ & =2\left[4 x-\frac{4 x^3}{3}\right]_0^1 \\ & =2\left(4-\frac{4}{3}\right)-(0-0)=2 \times \frac{8}{3} \\ & =\frac{16}{3} \text { sq units } \end{aligned} $$
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: The larger of the two areas (in sq. units) into which the circle x2+y2=16a2 is divided by the parabola y2=6ax, is
questions.solution:
For point of intersection, x2+6ax=16a2

⇒x=2a

Area=π⋅(4a)2−2(∫02a6axdx+∫2a4a16a2−x2dx)

=16πa2−2{(26a x⋅323)02a+(x216a2−x2+16a22sin−1x4a)2a4a}

=16πa2−2{26a⋅22 aa3+(8a2⋅π2−(a⋅23a+8a2⋅π6))}

=16πa2−2{83a23+8πa23−23a2}

 =32πa23−43a23=4a23(8π−3) sq.units
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: The area bounded by the curve $y=|x-2|, x=1$, $x=3$ and $\mathrm{X}$-axis is
questions.solution: $\begin{aligned} \text { Required area } & =\int_1^3|x-2| \mathrm{d} x \\ & =\int_1^2(2-x) \mathrm{d} x+\int_2^3(x-2) \mathrm{d} x \\ & =\left[2 x-\frac{x^2}{2}\right]_1^2+\left[\frac{x^2}{2}-2 x\right]_2^3 \\ & =\frac{1}{2}+\frac{1}{2}=1 \text { sq. unit }\end{aligned}$
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: The area (in sq. units) of the portion lying above the $X$-axis and enclosed between the curves $y^2=2 a x-x^2$ and $y^2=a x$ is
questions.solution:
Equation of given curves $x^2+y^2-2 a x=0$ and $y^2=a x$
$\because$ Area of the portion lying above the $X$-axis and enclosed between the given curves, from the diagram is $\int_0^a\left(\sqrt{2 a x-x^2}-\sqrt{a x}\right) d x$ $=\int_0^a \sqrt{a^2-(x-a)^2} d x-\sqrt{a} \int_0^a x^{1 / 2} d x$ $=\left[\frac{x-a}{2} \sqrt{2 a x-x^2}+\frac{a^2}{2} \sin ^{-1}\left(\frac{x-a}{a}\right)-\sqrt{a} \frac{2 x^{3 / 2}}{3}\right]_0^a$ $=-\frac{2}{3} a^2+\frac{\pi}{2} \frac{a^2}{2}=a^2\left(\frac{\pi}{4}-\frac{2}{3}\right)$ sq units
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: The area enclosed by the curves $y=8 x-x^2$ and $8 x-4 y+11=0$ is
questions.solution:
Given equation of curves are $$ \begin{aligned} & y=8 x-x^2 \\ & 8 x-4 y+11=0 \end{aligned} $$ Eq. (i) can be rewritten as $$ \begin{aligned} & y=-\left(x^2-2 \cdot 4 x+4^2-4^2\right) \\ & \Rightarrow \quad y=-\left[(x-4)^2-16\right] \\ & \Rightarrow \quad y-16=-(x-4)^2 \\ & \end{aligned} $$ $(x-4)^2=-(y-16)$, which represent a parabola open downward and having vertex at $(4,16)$. Clearly, Eq. (ii) represent a straight line passing through $\left(0, \frac{11}{4}\right)$ and $\left(-\frac{11}{8}, 0\right)$ Let us find the point of intersection of given curves, for this substitute the value of $y$ from Eq. (i) in Eq. (ii). $$ \begin{array}{rlrl} \Rightarrow & 8 x-4\left(8 x-x^2\right)+11 =0 \\ \Rightarrow & 8 x-32 x+4 x^2+11 =0 \\ \Rightarrow & 4 x^2-24 x+11 =0 \\ \Rightarrow & 4 x^2-22 x-2 x+11 =0 \\ \Rightarrow & 2 x(2 x-11)-1(2 x-11) =0 \\ \Rightarrow & (2 x-1)(2 x-11) =0 \\ \Rightarrow & x =\frac{1}{2} \text { or } \frac{11}{2} \end{array} $$ When $x=\frac{1}{2}$, then $y=\frac{15}{4}$ When $x=\frac{11}{2}$, then $y=\frac{55}{4} \quad$ [using Eq. (ii)] Now, required area $=$ Area of shaded region.
$$ =\int_{1 / 2}^{11 / 2}[y(\text { parabola })-y(\text { straight line })] d x $$ $$ \begin{aligned} & =\int_{1 / 2}^{11 / 2}\left[8 x-x^2-\left(\frac{8 x+11}{4}\right)\right] d x \\ & =\frac{1}{4} \int_{1 / 2}^{11 / 2}\left(32 x-4 x^2-8 x-11\right) d x \\ & =-\frac{1}{4} \int_{1 / 2}^{11 / 2}\left(4 x^2-24 x+11\right) d x \\ & =-\frac{1}{4}\left[\frac{4 x^3}{3}-24 \frac{x^2}{2}+11 x\right]_{1 / 2}^{11 / 2} \\ & =-\frac{1}{4}\left\{\left[\frac{4}{3} \cdot \frac{1331}{8}-12 \times \frac{121}{4}+\frac{121}{2}\right]-\left[\frac{1}{6}-3+\frac{11}{2}\right]\right\} \\ & =-\frac{1}{4}\left\{\frac{1331}{6}-363+\frac{121}{2}-\frac{1}{6}+3-\frac{11}{2}\right\} \\ & =\frac{-1}{4}\left\{\frac{1330}{6}-360+55\right\} \\ & =-\frac{1}{4}\left\{\frac{665}{3}-305\right\} \\ & =-\frac{1}{4}\left\{\frac{665-915}{3}\right\}=\frac{250}{4 \times 3}=\frac{125}{6} \text { sq units } \end{aligned} $$
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: The area (in square units) of the region enclosed by the two circles $x^2+y^2=1$ and $(x-1)^2+y^2=1$ is
questions.solution:
Intersection point of two circles
$x^2+y^2=1$ $\ldots$ (i) $(x-1)^2+y^2=1$ $\ldots$ (ii) is given by $(x-1)^2+\left(1-x^2\right)=1$ $\Rightarrow \quad x^2+1-2 x-x^2=0$ $\Rightarrow \quad x=\frac{1}{2}$ From Eq. (i), $\frac{1}{4}+y^2=1$ $y^2=1-\frac{1}{4} \Rightarrow y= \pm \frac{\sqrt{3}}{2}$ Point $A\left(\frac{1}{2}, \frac{\sqrt{3}}{2}\right)$ and $C\left(\frac{1}{2}, \frac{-\sqrt{3}}{2}\right)$ So, Area of region $O A B C O$ $=2 \times$ Area of region $O A B D O$ Area of $O A B D O$ $=$ Area of $O A D O+$ Area of $A B D A$ $=\int_0^{1 / 2} \sqrt{1-(x-1)^2} d x+\int_{1 / 2}^1 \sqrt{1-x^2} d x$ $=\left[\frac{1}{2} \cdot(x-1) \sqrt{1-(x-1)^2}+\frac{1}{2} \sin ^{-1}\left(\frac{x-1}{1}\right)\right]_0^{1 / 2}$ $+\left[\frac{1}{2} x \sqrt{1-x^2}+\frac{1}{2} \sin ^{-1}\left(\frac{x}{1}\right)\right]_{1 / 2}^1$ $=\left[-\frac{1}{2} \cdot \frac{1}{2} \cdot \frac{\sqrt{3}}{2}+\frac{1}{2} \sin ^{-1}\left(\frac{-1}{2}\right)-\frac{1}{2} \cdot 0-\frac{1}{2} \sin ^{-1}(-1)\right]$ $+\left[\frac{1}{2} \cdot 0+\frac{1}{2} \sin ^{-1}(1)-\frac{1}{4} \cdot \frac{\sqrt{3}}{2}-\frac{1}{2} \sin ^{-1}\left(\frac{1}{2}\right)\right]$ $=\left(-\frac{\sqrt{3}}{8}\right)-\frac{1}{2} \sin ^{-1}\left(\frac{1}{2}\right)+\frac{1}{2} \sin ^{-1}(1)$ $+\frac{1}{2} \sin ^{-1}(1)-\frac{\sqrt{3}}{8}-\frac{1}{2} \sin ^{-1}\left(\frac{1}{2}\right)$ $=\sin ^{-1}(1)-\sin ^{-1}\left(\frac{1}{2}\right)-\frac{\sqrt{3}}{4}$ $=\frac{\pi}{2}-\frac{\pi}{6}-\frac{\sqrt{3}}{4}$ $=\left(\frac{\pi}{3}-\frac{\sqrt{3}}{4}\right)$ from Eq. (i), Area of region $O A B C O=2 \times\left(\frac{\pi}{3}-\frac{\sqrt{3}}{4}\right)$ $=\left(\frac{2 \pi}{3}-\frac{\sqrt{3}}{2}\right)$
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: If the area of the region bounded by $y=\cos x$, $y=\sin x, x=\frac{\pi}{4}$ and $x=\pi$ is bisected by the line $x=a$, then $\sin \left(a+\frac{\pi}{4}\right)=$
questions.solution: According to the question, $\int_{\frac{\pi}{4}}^a(\sin x-\cos x) d x=\int_a^\pi(\sin x-\cos x) d x$ $\begin{aligned} {[-\cos x-\sin x]_{\frac{\pi}{4}}^a } & =[-\cos x-\sin x]_a^\pi \\ -\cos a & -\sin a+\frac{1}{\sqrt{2}}+\frac{1}{\sqrt{2}}=1+\cos a+\sin a \\ \sqrt{2}-1 & =2(\cos a+\sin a) \\ \frac{\sqrt{2}-1}{2} & =\sin a+\cos a \\ \frac{\sqrt{2}-1}{2 \sqrt{2}} & =\frac{1}{\sqrt{2}} \sin a+\frac{1}{\sqrt{2}} \cos a \\ \frac{\sqrt{2}-1}{2 \sqrt{2}} & =\sin a \cdot \cos \frac{\pi}{4}+\cos a \sin \frac{\pi}{4} \\ \frac{\sqrt{2}-1}{2 \sqrt{2}} & =\sin \left(a+\frac{\pi}{4}\right)\end{aligned}$
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: The area (in sq. units) bounded by the parabola $y=x^2+3$, the tangent to the parabola at $(3,12)$ and the coordinate axes and lying in the first quadrant is
questions.solution:
Given parabola, $y=x^2+3$ Tangent of parabola at $(3,12)$ is $\frac{Y+12}{2}=3 x+3 \Rightarrow Y=6 x-6$
$\begin{aligned} \text { Required area } & =\int_0^3\left(x^2+3\right) d x-\int_1^3(6 x-6) d x \\ & =\left[\frac{x^3}{3}+3 x\right]_0^3-\left[3 x^2-6 x\right]_1^3 \\ & =(9+9)-((27-18)+3)=18-12=6\end{aligned}$
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: The area (in square units) of the region bounded by $x^2=8 y, x=4$ and $X$-axis, is
questions.solution:
Given equations are $\begin{aligned} x^2 & =8 y \\ x & =4 \\ y & =0\end{aligned}$ and
On solving Eqs. (i) and (ii), we get $(4,2)$ The required area $=\int_0^4 y d x=\int_0^4 \frac{x^2}{8} d x=\left[\frac{x^3}{24}\right]_0^4=\frac{64}{24}=\frac{8}{3}$ The required area $=\frac{8}{3}$ sq units
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: The area enclosed (in square units) by the curve <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>y</mi><mo>=</mo><msup><mi>x</mi><mn>4</mn></msup><mo>-</mo><msup><mi>x</mi><mn>2</mn></msup></math>, the <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>x</mi></math>-axis and the vertical lines passing through the two minimum points of the curve is
questions.solution:
Given that y=x4-x2

For minimum points of curve:

ddxx4-x2=0

4x3-2x=0

2x2x2-1=0

⇒2x=0 or 2x2-1=0

x=0 or x=±12

So f is minimum at ±12

The area =∫-1212x4-x2dx 

=2∫012x4-x2dx  ∵ fx  is even function

=2x55-x33120

=2125·15-123·13

=2142·15-122·13

=222110-13

=3-10302

∴ Area =7302
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: The area (in sq unit) of the region bounded by the curves $2 x=y^2-1$ and $x=0$ is
questions.solution:
Given curve can be rewritten as $$ y^2=2\left(x+\frac{1}{2}\right) $$
$\therefore$ Required area $=\int_{-1}^1 x d y$ $\begin{aligned} & =2 \int_0^1 \frac{y^2-1}{2} d y \\ & =\left|\left[\frac{y^3}{3}-y\right]_0^1\right| \\ & =\left|\left[\frac{1}{3}-1\right]\right|=\frac{2}{3} \text { sq unit }\end{aligned}$
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q:
The values of a function $f(x)$ at different values of $x$ are as follows
Then, the approximate area (in square units) bounded by the curve $y=f(x)$ and $x$-axis between $x=0$ and 5 , using the Trapezoidal rule, is
questions.solution: $h=$ difference of two values of $x$ Take value of $f(x)$ as $\left(y_0, y_1, y_2, \ldots, y_5\right)$ Then by Trapezoidal rule Now, $\int_{x_0}^{x_0+n h} f(x) d x$ $=\frac{h}{2}\left[\left(y_0+y_5\right)+2\left(y_1+y_2+y_3+y_4\right)\right]$ $=\frac{1}{2}[(2+27)+2(3+6+11+18)]$ $=\frac{1}{2}[29+2 \cdot 38]=\frac{1}{2}(29+76)$ $=\frac{1}{2} \times 105=52.5$ Approximate area $=52.5 \mathrm{sq}$ unit
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: If the area of the region enclosed by the curve $x^2+y^2=16$ and then lines $x=2$ and $x=3$ is $\left(3 \sqrt{7}-4 \sqrt{3}-\frac{8 \pi}{3}+k\right)$ sq units, then $k$ equals
questions.solution:
Area of region enclosed by the curve $x^2+y^2=16$ and line $x=2$ and $x=3$ is
$$
\begin{aligned}
& A=\int_2^3 \sqrt{16-x^2} d x \\
& A=\left[\frac{x}{2} \sqrt{16-x^2}+8 \sin ^{-1} \frac{x}{4}\right]_2^3 \\
& \Rightarrow 3 \sqrt{7}-4 \sqrt{3}-\frac{8 \pi}{3}+k \\
& =\left(\frac{3}{2} \sqrt{7}+8 \sin ^{-1} \frac{3}{4}\right)-\left(2 \sqrt{3}+8 \sin ^{-1} \frac{2}{4}\right) \\
& \Rightarrow 3 \sqrt{7}-4 \sqrt{3}-\frac{8 \pi}{3}+k \\
& =\frac{3}{2} \sqrt{7}+8 \sin ^{-1} \frac{3}{4}-2 \sqrt{3}-\frac{8 \pi}{6} \\
& =\frac{1}{2}\left(3 \sqrt{7}+16 \sin ^{-1} \frac{3}{4}-4 \sqrt{3}-\frac{8 \pi}{3}\right) \\
& \therefore \quad k=16 \sin ^{-1}\left(\frac{3}{4}\right) \\
&
\end{aligned}
$$
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: The area bounded by $y-1=-|x|$ and $y+1=|x|$ is
questions.solution:
The given curves are $$ \begin{aligned} & y-1=-|x| \\ & y+1=|x| \end{aligned} $$
Here, $\mathrm{AC}=\sqrt{1+1}=\sqrt{2}$ Area of ACBD $=4$ Area of AOC $=4 \times \frac{1}{2} \times O \mathrm{OAOC}=4 \times \frac{1}{2} \times 1 \times 1=2$ $\therefore$ Area of the required bounded region is 2 .
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: The area bounded by the curve $x=\log (|y|)$, the lines $x=-1$ and $x=0$ is
questions.solution:
Given the curve $x=\log |y|$ Graph of curves
Required area $=2 \int_{-1}^0 e^x d x=2\left(e^x\right)_{-1}^0=2\left(1-e^{-1}\right)$
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: The area (in square units) bounded by the curves $y=2 x^2$ and $y=\max \{x-[x], x+|x|\}$ in between the lines $x=0$ and $x=2$ is
questions.solution:
$$ \begin{aligned} & y=\max \cdot\{x-[x], \quad x+|x|\} \\ & \Rightarrow \quad y= \begin{cases}2 x, & x \geq 0 \\ \{x\}, & x < 0\end{cases} \end{aligned} $$ Shaded region is required Area $$ =\left|\int_0^1\left(2 x-2 x^2\right) d x\right|+\left|\int_1^2\left(2 x^2-2 x\right) d x\right| $$
$\begin{aligned} & =\left|\int_0^1 2 x d x-\int_0^1 2 x^2 d x\right|+\left|\int_1^2 2 x^2 d x-\int_1^2 2 x d x\right| \\ & =\left|\left[\frac{2 x^2}{2}\right]_0^1-\left[\frac{2 x^3}{3}\right]_0^1\right|+\left|\left[\frac{2 x^3}{3}\right]_1^2-\left[\frac{2 x^2}{2}\right]_1^2\right| \\ & =\left|1-\frac{2}{3}\right|+\left|\frac{16}{3}-\frac{2}{3}-4+1\right|=\frac{1}{3}+\frac{5}{3}=2 \text { sq. units. }\end{aligned}$
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: Area bounded by the lines $y=x, x=-1, x=2$ and $x$ -axis is
questions.solution:
Required area $=\int_{-1}^{2} y d x$
$=\int_{-1}^{0} y d x+\int_{0}^{2} y d x$ $=\left|\int_{-1}^{0} x d x\right|+\int_{0}^{2} x d x$ $\left.=\mid \frac{x^{2}}{2}\right]_{1}^{0} \mid+\left[\frac{x^{2}}{2}\right]_{0}^{2}=\frac{5}{2}$ sq unit
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: The volume of solid generated by revolving about the $y$ -axis the figure bounded by the parabola $y=x^{2}$ and $x=y^{2}$ is
questions.solution:
$V=\int_{0}^{1} \pi x^{2} d y$
$=\left|\pi \int_{0}^{1}\left(y^{4}-y\right) d y\right|$ $=\left|\pi\left[\frac{y^{5}}{5}-\frac{y^{2}}{2}\right]_{0}^{1}\right|$ $=\left|\pi\left[\frac{1}{5}-\frac{1}{3}\right]\right|$ $=\left|\frac{-2 \pi}{15}\right|=\frac{2 \pi}{15}$
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: The area enclosed by the curves $y=x|x|$, $x=-1$ and $x=1$ is.......... sq units.
questions.solution:
Given, curves are $y=x|x|=\left\{\begin{array}{cc}x^2 & ; \quad x \geq 0 \\ -x^2 & ; \quad x < 0\end{array}\right.$ $x=-1$ and $x=1$
We have plotted the figure and shaded regions are the area enclosed by the curves and area of 1 first part $=$ Area of II second part. $A_1=\int_0^1 x^2 d x=\left[\frac{x^3}{3}\right]_0^1=\frac{1}{3}$ Similarly, $A_{11}=\frac{1}{3}$ $\therefore \quad A=\frac{1}{3}+\frac{1}{3}=\frac{2}{3}$
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: Area bounded between the curve $x^{2}=y$ and the line $y=4 x$ is
questions.solution:
Given curves are $x^{2}=y$ and $y=4 x$
Intersection points are $(0,0)$ and $(4,16)$ $\begin{aligned} \therefore \text { Required area } &=\int_{0}^{4}\left(4 x-x^{2}\right) d x \\ &=\left[\frac{4 x^{2}}{2}-\frac{x^{3}}{3}\right]_{0}^{4} \\ &=\left[32-\frac{64}{3}\right] \\ &=\frac{32}{3} \mathrm{sq} \text { unit } \end{aligned}$
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: The volume of the solid formed by rotating the area enclosed between the curve $y^{2}=4 x, x=4$ and $x=5$ about $x$ -axis is (in cubic units)
questions.solution:
Volume of the solid $=\int_{4}^{5} \pi y^{2} d x$
$=\pi \int_{4}^{5} 4 x d x$
$=4 \pi\left[\frac{x^{2}}{2}\right]_{4}^{5}$
$=2 x(25-16)$
$=18 \pi$ cu unit
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q:
The area of the region bounded by the curves $x^{2}+y^{2}=8$ and
$y^{2}=2 x$ is
questions.solution:
Given curves, $$ \begin{aligned} x^{2}+y^{2} &=8...(i)\\ and\quad y^{2} &=2 x...(ii) \end{aligned} $$
On solving Eqs. (i) and (ii), we get $\therefore$ $$ \begin{aligned} x^{2}+2 x-8 &=0 \\ x^{2}+4 x-2 x-8 &=0 \\ x(x+4)-2(x+4) &=0 \\ (x-2)(x+4) &=0 \\ x=2 \text { and } y &=\pm 2 \end{aligned} $$ $\therefore$ Required area $$ \begin{array}{l} =2[\text { Area of } O A P+\text { Area of } P A B] \\ =2\left[\int_{0}^{2} \sqrt{2 x} d x+\int_{2}^{2 \sqrt{2}} \sqrt{8-x^{2}} d x\right] \\ =2\left[\sqrt{2}\left(x^{3 / 2} \cdot \frac{2}{3}\right)_{0}^{2}+\left(\frac{x}{2} \sqrt{8-x^{2}}\right.\right. \\ \left.\left.+\frac{8}{2} \sin ^{-1} \frac{x}{2 \sqrt{2}}\right)_{2}^{2 \sqrt{2}}\right] \\ =2\left[\frac{2 \sqrt{2}}{3}\left(2^{3 / 2}\right)+4 \times \frac{\pi}{2}-2-4 \times \frac{\pi}{4}\right] \end{array} $$ $=2\left[\frac{2 \sqrt{2}}{3} \cdot 2 \sqrt{2}+2 \pi-2-\pi\right]$ $=2\left[\frac{8}{3}-2+\pi\right]=2\left(\frac{2}{3}+\pi\right)=2 \pi+\frac{4}{3}$
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: The area bounded by the parabola $y^{2}=x$, straight line $y=4$ and $y$ -axis is square units is
questions.solution:
Line $y=4$ meets the parabola $y^{2}=x$ at $A$ $\therefore \quad 16=x$ and $A$ is $(16,4)$
$\therefore$ Required area $$ \begin{array}{l} =\int_{y=0}^{4} x d y \\ =\int_{y=0}^{4} y^{2} d y \\ =\left[y^{3} / 3\right]_{0}^{4} \\ =\frac{64}{3} \text { sq units } \end{array} $$
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: The area of the region bounded by the curves, $y^{2}=8 x$ and $y=x$ is
questions.solution:
Given curves, $$ y^{2}=8x...(i) $$ and $$ y=x...(ii) $$
On solving Eqs. (i) and (ii), we get $\begin{aligned} x^{2}-8 x &=0 \\ x(x-8) &=0 \\ \Rightarrow \quad x &=0,8 \end{aligned}$ and $\quad y=0,8$ $\begin{aligned} \therefore \text { Required area, }(O P A) &=\int_{0}^{8}(\sqrt{8 x}-x) d x \\ &=\left[2 \sqrt{2} \cdot \frac{2}{3} \cdot x^{3 / 2}-\frac{x^{2}}{2}\right]_{0}^{8} \end{aligned}$ $=\frac{4 \sqrt{2}}{3} \cdot(8)^{3 / 2}-\frac{(8)^{2}}{2}$ $=\frac{4 \sqrt{2}}{3} \cdot(2)^{3} \cdot 2 \sqrt{2}-\frac{64}{2}$ $=\frac{16}{3} \times 8-\frac{64}{2}=64\left(\frac{2}{3}-\frac{1}{2}\right)$ $=64 \times \frac{(4-3)}{6}=64 \times \frac{1}{6}=\frac{32}{3}$
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.solution:
A=∫02 2x-x2dx
⇒  A=x2-x3302
⇒  A=4-83
⇒  A=43 sq units

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: If a curve $y=\mathrm{a} \sqrt{x}+\mathrm{b} x$ passes through the point $(1,2)$ and the area bounded by the curve, line $x=4$ and $\mathrm{X}$-axis is 8 sq. units, then
questions.solution:
The given curve passes through $(1,2)$.
$\therefore \quad 2=a+b$
According to the given condition,
$\int_0^4(a \sqrt{x}+b x) d x=8$                    ...(i)
$\Rightarrow \frac{2 \mathrm{a}}{3}\left[x^{3 / 2}\right]_0^4+\frac{\mathrm{b}}{2}\left[x^2\right]_0^4=8 \Rightarrow \frac{2 \mathrm{a}}{3} \cdot 8+8 \mathrm{~b}=8$
$\Rightarrow 2 a+3 b=3$             ...(ii)
From (i) and (ii), we get
$\mathrm{a}=3, \mathrm{~b}=-1$
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: Find the area enclosed between the curves <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msup><mi mathvariant="normal">x</mi><mn>2</mn></msup><mo>+</mo><msup><mi mathvariant="normal">y</mi><mn>2</mn></msup><mo>≤</mo><mn>9</mn><msup><mi mathvariant="normal">π</mi><mn>2</mn></msup></math> and <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>sin</mi><mo>⁡</mo><mfenced separators="|"><mrow><mi mathvariant="normal">x</mi><mi mathvariant="normal"> </mi><mo>-</mo><mi mathvariant="normal"> </mi><mi mathvariant="normal">y</mi></mrow></mfenced><mo>≥</mo><mn>0</mn></math>.
questions.solution:
The given curves are x2+y2≤9π2 and sinx-y≥0.

 So, the required area is

A=π3π2/2

Hence, required area is 9π32.
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: The area (in sq. units) of the smaller part of the circle $x^2+y^2=\mathrm{a}^2$ cut off by the line $x=\frac{\mathrm{a}}{\sqrt{2}}$ is
questions.solution: Substitute $x=\frac{\mathrm{a}}{\sqrt{2}}$ in $x^2+y^2=\mathrm{a}^2$, we get $\frac{\mathrm{a}^2}{2}+y^2=\mathrm{a}^2 \Rightarrow y= \pm \frac{\mathrm{a}}{\sqrt{2}}$ $\therefore \quad$ Required area $$ \begin{aligned} & =2 \int_{\frac{a}{\sqrt{2}}}^a \sqrt{a^2-x^2} d x \\ & =2\left[\frac{x}{2} \sqrt{a^2-x^2}+\frac{a^2}{2} \sin ^{-1}\left(\frac{x}{a}\right)\right]_{\frac{a}{\sqrt{2}}}^2 \\ & =2\left\{\left[0+\frac{a^2}{2} \times \frac{\pi}{2}\right]-\left[\frac{a}{2 \sqrt{2}} \sqrt{a^2-\frac{a^2}{2}}+\frac{a^2}{2} \times \frac{\pi}{4}\right]\right\} \\ & =2\left[\frac{a^2 \pi}{4}-\frac{a^2}{4}-\frac{a^2 \pi}{8}\right] \\ & =\frac{a^2}{2}\left|\pi-1-\frac{\pi}{2}\right| \\ & =\frac{a^2}{2}\left|\frac{\pi}{2}-1\right| \end{aligned} $$
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: What is the area of loop of the curve <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>r</mi><mo>=</mo><mi>a</mi><mi>sin</mi><mn>3</mn><mi>θ</mi></math>?
questions.solution:
If curve r=asin3θ, to trace the curve, we consider the following table:

3θ= 0 π2 π 3π2 2π 5π2 3π
θ= 0 π6 π3 π2 2π3 5π6 π
r= 0 a 0 -a 0 a 0

Thus, there is a loop between θ=0 to θ=π3  and r varies from r=0 to r=a.

      

Hence, the area of the loop lying in the positive
quadrant

=12∫0π3r2dθ

=a22∫0π3sin23θdθ

On putting, 3θ=ϕ⇒dθ=13dϕ, we get

=a22∫0πsin2ϕ·13 dϕ

=a26∫0πsin2ϕdϕ

=a26·2·∫0π2sin2ϕdϕ

=a26·2·π4

=πa212 sq. units.
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
